title: NASA enters final countdown for first Moon mission in 50 years
description: Final preparations are being made for NASA’s first mission to the moon in more than half a century.  The space agency says there is an 80 per cent chance of the mission going ahead based on weather data.  The mission will send a crew of four astronauts on a historic 10-day flyby mission around the moon.

title: Donald Trump unleashes on the United Kingdom, warns the US will not 'help you anymore'
description: US President Donald Trump has slammed longtime ally the United Kingdom in a scathing post to Truth Social.  On Tuesday, Trump claimed the UK should build up “some courage” before stating the nation needs to learn “how to fight” for itself.  The president concluded his brutal post by warning Britain that the US will not “be there to help you anymore” and for the UK to “get your own oil”.  Trump’s remarks follow a degradation of US and UK relations after Britain refused to allow American aircraft to use its airbase during Operation Epic Fury.

title: ‘Money’s not free’: Former NSW premier condemns Albanese government spending
description: Former New South Wales premier Mike Baird has hit out at the Albanese government’s spending by claiming Australia is in a “structural deficit”.  “Australia's always had low debt versus the rest of the world, and we still are, but we have deteriorated,” Mr Baird told Sky News Australia.    “I think that every budget should have a ten-year projection.  "Money’s not free; there are costs and consequences.”

title: Airfreight prices soar while ASX reaches its highest level in weeks
description: The Drewry Airfreight Index has surged 95 per cent over the past month, driven by reduced flight traffic over Dubai and rising fuel costs, including higher surcharges.  Markets are also pricing in a 65 per cent likelihood of an interest rate increase in May.  Meanwhile, the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX) climbed 1.56 per cent on Wednesday, reaching its highest level in two to three weeks.

title: US journalist kidnapped in Iraq amid suspected militant involvement
description: American journalist Shelly Kittleson was reportedly kidnapped by Iran-backed militant group Kataib Hezbollah in the Iraqi capital Baghdad.  An arrest was made following footage that was found showing armed men dragging her into the back of a car.  Her abduction was confirmed by Al-Monitor, an independent media outlet she contributed to.

title: ‘Apocalyptic vision’: Rubio reveals why US launched strikes on Iran
description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio outlines the Trump administration's justification for launching military action against Iran.  “We were on the verge of an Iran that had so many missiles and so many drones that no one could do anything about its nuclear weapons program in the future,” Mr Rubio said.  “That was an intolerable risk. Under no circumstances can a country run by radical Shia clerics with an apocalyptic vision of the future ever possess nuclear weapons.”

title: Trump blasts judge who halted construction of the ballroom
description: US President Donald Trump blasts a federal judge for halting the construction of the ballroom.  “This has been financed privately; it’s a donation that’s been given,” Mr Trump said.  “For 150 years, they’ve wanted a ballroom here; we’re going to have the finest ballroom probably in the world.  “This is taxpayer-free.”

title: ‘All over the map’: Trump’s Iran war timeline under fire
description: Former US Army lieutenant colonel Daniel Davis believes the “evidence on the ground” doesn't support US President Donald Trump’s claim that the Iran war will end in two to three weeks.  “He's been saying all kinds of stuff that's literally all over the map, to include, they’ve already been defeated, we're just cleaning it up … then there's a 48-hour ultimatum, and then a 5-day ultimatum,” Mr Davis told Sky News Australia.  “You never know where he's going to go next. What we do know is that there continues to be the deployment of ground forces in the area.  “I look more at what's happening on the ground than I do at what's being said in Washington.”

title: ‘Vance’s charms are alluding many Americans’: CPAC straw poll result a ‘warning sign’ for VP
description: Political journalist John Fund has analysed the straw poll at the 2026 Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC), which US Vice President JD Vance topped.  “I would have expected the only man that Donald Trump has anointed with real political power at the national level, the Vice President, didn’t do better,” he told Sky News host James Bolt.  “I think that’s a warning sign that JD Vance’s charms are alluding many Americans.”

title: ‘Overwhelmingly elderly and white’: ‘Manufactured’ No Kings protest against Trump
description: Political journalist John Fund has analysed the No Kings protest, which took place over the weekend in America.  Millions of Americans took to the streets across the United States to protest US President Donald Trump’s policies and the war with Iran.  More than 3,000 rallies were held in all 50 states as part of the latest ‘No Kings’ protest marches.  “The crowd is overwhelmingly elderly and white,” Mr Fund told Sky News host James Bolt.

title: Outrage as Keir Starmer welcomes former Al-Qaeda leader to the UK
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is facing major backlash after hosting Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa in London.  Al-Sharaa, who previously had ties to groups linked to Al-Qaeda, met Starmer to discuss migration and security cooperation.  Critics say the move raises serious questions about legitimising controversial figures on British soil.  The meeting focused on border security and tackling illegal migration routes into Europe.  The controversy adds to growing pressure on Starmer over his handling of foreign policy and national security.

title: Kristi Noem left 'devastated' after husband's shock cross-dressing double life exposed
description: Former US DHS chief Kristi Noem has released a statement addressing reports of her husband's alleged involvement in fetish communities and cross-dressing.  Former Department of Homeland Security chief Kristi Noem is “devastated” by allegations her husband Bryon lives a double life where he cross-dresses and chats online with fetish models.  According to reporting by the Daily Mail, Bryon Noem chatted up women from the so-called “bimbofication” fetish scene, in which adult performers augment their breasts with massive amounts of saline.  The Daily Mail reports that Noem’s husband enthusiastically praised the appearances of three women, citing "hundreds" of messages they purportedly sent from the scene.  According to the publication, he also proclaimed that he coveted “huge, huge ridiculous boobs".  Bryon Noem allegedly sent women approximately $US25,000 via Cash App and PayPal as he admired their over-the-top bust sizes and “amazing” curves, the news outlet reported.  One of the models claimed she discovered his true identity after butt-dialing him and heard a voicemail greeting saying “Noem Insurance, leave a message".  One photo the Mail claims Bryon shared with the women features him wearing pink hot-pants and a flesh-colored suit, using "balloons" to mimic comically oversized breasts.  Kristi Noem’s representatives told The New York Post that she is "devastated".  “Ms. Noem is devastated. The family was blindsided by this, and they ask for privacy and prayers at the time,” Noem’s representatives told The Post.

title: Middle East war is having ‘widespread ramifications’ for Australia
description: Housing Minister Clare O’Neil says the war in the Middle East is having “widespread ramifications” for Australia.

title: Trump savagely declares the US will abandon disloyal NATO allies after Iran war bust-up
description: Donald Trump is not forgiving or forgetting how his NATO allies treated the United States over the past four weeks of the Iran war.

title: 'The View' co-hosts launch vicious attack on Usha Vance, claim she's ‘addicted' to power
description: ‘The View’ co-hosts lave launched an unhinged attack at Second Lady Usha Vance and accused the pregnant mother of three of being “addicted to power”.  The co-host’s remarks followed Usha’s Monday interview on NBC News morning show ‘Today’, where she asked about her past as a Democrat.  Sunny Hostin argued Usha’s views should align with Americans as the country is in an “existential crisis”.  Joy Behar pointed out that not only was Usha a former Democrat, but her husband, Vice President JD Vance, was once against Trump.  The co-host attacked both Usha and JD for switching alliances to the Republican Party and are now “kissing Trump’s butt”.  Behar then claimed the Vances’ change of heart came down to “money and power”.
